name: Sour Cream Chocolate Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a large mixing bowl.
2. Add the shortening, sour cream, vanilla, eggs, melted and cooled chocolate (melted in microwave), and water to the dry ingredients. Beat with an electric mixer at low speed until combined.
3. Scrape the sides of the bowl constantly and increase the speed to high. Mix for 1/2 minute more.
4. Pour the batter into two greased, 9-inch round cake pans lined with wax paper.
5. Bake in a preheated oven set to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
6. Allow cooling in the pans on racks for 10 minutes before removing the cakes and placing them on wire racks to cool completely.
7. To assemble, place one cake layer onto a serving plate. Spread with sour cream/chocolate frosting.
8. Place the second cake layer on top, right side up. Frost the sides and top of the cake with remaining frosting.
9. For best results, trim any layers that rise less to create a flat surface before assembling.
Sour Cream/Chocolate Frosting:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine softened butter (or regular margarine), confectioners' sugar, and sour cream until well blended.
2. Add melted and cooled chocolate, then vanilla extract. Beat until smooth.
